$about: Manually adjudicated replication edges + stub Bob declarations that the wiki-CSV miner cannot infer alone. `bobs` is a list of {name, note} entries for Bobs known to exist in prose but with parent unknown (typically gen-8+ Bobs whose parentage tracks under issue #5). `edges` matches data/edges_replication.json. Merged by scripts/consolidate_bobs.py after the auto-mined edges. Each entry MUST cite the chapter and the prose evidence.

bobs.name: Mack
bobs.note: B3 C18 chapter (Mack POV): fleet commander arriving at 82 Eridani with three colony ships piloted by Isaac, Jack, and Owen. The earlier #4 reading that 'three clones popped in' implied Mack-as-parent has been overturned by #8 — Mack treats Isaac/Jack/Owen as peers in his VR, and his surprise at their shared variation argues against him being their progenitor. Mack's own parentage remains unattributed in prose. Gen-8+ extraction was tracked under issue #5; this stub remains for the bob to exist in the graph.

bobs.name: Will
bobs.note: Active Bob from B3 onward (e.g. Sol-system manager; by B4 retired to Valhalla). Own parentage not stated in prose; absent from genealogy.json (which covers ~through B2) and from google-sheet-genealogy.csv. Bootstrapped so the Will→Conan edge (B4 P1 C7) resolves cleanly. Gen-8+ extraction tracked under issue #5.

bobs.name: Jack
bobs.note: B3 C18: pilots one of three colony ships under Mack's fleet arriving at 82 Eridani, alongside Isaac and Owen. The B3 C18 prose 'Isaac, Jack, and Owen popped in. … the same variation in three clones at the same time' establishes they are clones (of some shared parent), not specifically Mack's. With B3 C21 explicitly attributing Isaac to Bart, Jack/Owen are *plausibly* Bart's clones by cohort inference — but no direct prose attribution. Demoted to parent unknown under #8 per strict-prose-only rule.

bobs.name: Owen
bobs.note: B3 C18: pilots one of three colony ships under Mack's fleet arriving at 82 Eridani, alongside Isaac and Jack. Same provenance as Jack — clone of unknown parent, plausibly Bart's by cohort with Isaac but not explicitly attributed in prose. Demoted to parent unknown under #8 per strict-prose-only rule.

edges.source_text: This particular replicant, Isaac, was one of Bart's early clones, senior even to Oliver.
edges.note: B3 C21 explicit attribution. Overturns #4's earlier Isaac←Mack reading (which inferred parentage from Mack's fleet command in B3 C18). Adjudicated under issue #8.

edges.source_text: "What can I do for you, O great ancestor?" Conan said. "Are you one of mine?" I replied, and he nodded.
edges.note: B4 P1 C7 (Will POV) explicit: Will asks Conan 'Are you one of mine?' — Conan nods. Adjudicated under issue #5.
